synopsis: The Standing Judicial Commission denied a complaint by Dan and Angelia Crouse challenging Northwest Georgia Presbytery's affirmation of a Session's decision to not advance Crouse's nomination for re-election as Deacon. The SJC held that Sessions possess broad discretion in determining officer eligibility and that absent clear constitutional error or unconstitutional action, such discretionary decisions should not be disturbed by higher courts.
notes: Case decided July 21, 2020 (reported in GA 48, 2021). Vote: 18 concur, 2 dissent (Carrell, Nusbaum), 9 absent. Decision written by RE Jack Wilson, revised and approved by Panel and full SJC. Two-page dissenting opinion (pp. 810-813) argues the SJC should have applied the stricter standard of review for constitutional interpretation issues (BCO 39-3.4) rather than the deference standard for discretionary matters. This is a follow-up case to 2019-03 (Crouse v. NWGP), involving the same complainants and same presbytery regarding a different nomination review.
